natural_language: Five blocks — call them A through E — are arranged in two distinct piles. The left pile has C resting on B, and B resting on A, with A on the table. So left pile from bottom to top goes: A, B, C. The right pile is a short one: D is on E, and E is on the table. The topmost surfaces belong to C on the left and D on the right. The gripper is currently empty. We need to merge everything into one single column. The desired ordering from the table upward: C should be at the very bottom, then D on top of C, then E above D, then B above E, with A at the summit. So the final column reads bottom-to-top as C, D, E, B, A.
